templates.text: You have meaningful AI spend with low unit economics visibility. Most teams in your position uncover 20–40% avoidable cost by optimizing prompts, model choice, and routing.
You indicated margin pressure or budget scrutiny as a key problem. A full AI Cost Audit will map exactly where costs are accumulating and provide a prioritised savings plan.
Premium models for simple tasks are a common leakage pattern. Systematic model tiering — routing cheaper models for low-complexity tasks — typically yields 15–30% savings without quality loss.
Large prompts and context windows often drive significant token waste. Targeted prompt tuning and context pruning is usually the fastest win in a cost audit.
Weak caching and routing strategies mean you are paying for redundant inference. Implementing semantic caching and intelligent routing can reduce API call volume by 20–50%.
Idle or overprovisioned GPU infrastructure is a silent budget drain. Right-sizing reserved compute to actual utilisation patterns typically recovers 20–35% of infrastructure spend.
Unattributed AI spend across teams and features makes it impossible to optimise. Establishing cost tagging and attribution is the essential first step — and it surfaces the biggest surprises.
You haven't yet run structured optimisation across prompts, models, routing, and infra. Our deep audit is designed to do exactly that — giving you a prioritised, evidence-based savings roadmap.
You said ≥25% savings would make a deeper audit an obvious yes. Based on similar stacks — especially those with limited prompt tuning or model tiering — that target is realistic.
You need visibility before committing to a savings target — which is exactly where an audit starts. The first deliverable is a complete cost breakdown by model, team, and feature.

tierDefaults.1: Based on your profile, a full AI Cost Audit is highly likely to surface significant savings. Most teams at your stage of AI dependence and spend recover 20–40% of costs through structured optimisation.
tierDefaults.2: Your profile indicates solid optimisation potential. A scoped audit focused on your highest-risk areas — prompts, model selection, or routing — would give you a clear, actionable savings roadmap.
tierDefaults.3: Your AI cost profile is relatively healthy for your current stage. As usage scales, maintaining unit economics visibility and a structured review cadence will keep costs from compounding.
tierDefaults.4: You're in the early stages of AI adoption — the right time to build cost-aware habits. We'll share content on unit economics, model selection, and cost governance as you move toward production workloads.
